title: Operations Strategy and Competitiveness
description: Assesses strategic alignment, operations decision making, competitive priorities, order winners and qualifiers, and productivity analysis.
subject: DSC2370 Operations Management

questions.question: Which statement best captures why operations strategy is considered a functional strategy rather than a corporate strategy?
options: It focuses on internal processes that support higher level strategic objectives
It defines the long term financial targets of the organization
It determines which industries the firm should exit or enter
It allocates capital among unrelated business units
questions.answer: It focuses on internal processes that support higher level strategic objectives
questions.rationale: Operations strategy narrows scope to how operations resources and processes support broader strategies.

questions.question: A misalignment between competitive priorities and competitive capabilities most directly indicates which managerial problem?
options: A gap between what customers value and what operations can deliver
Over investment in non core activities
questions.answer: A gap between what customers value and what operations can deliver
questions.rationale: Competitive priorities reflect market expectations while capabilities reflect actual performance.

questions.question: Which of the following decisions is most clearly classified at the strategic level of operations decision making?
options: Assigning workers to shifts for next week
Determining the geographic location of a new manufacturing plant
Sequencing jobs on a production line today
Authorizing overtime for the current month
questions.answer: Determining the geographic location of a new manufacturing plant
questions.rationale: Facility location decisions have long term impact and are strategic.

questions.question: Why is market segmentation a critical input to deriving an operations strategy?
options: It reduces the need for capacity planning
It clarifies which customer needs operations must prioritize
It eliminates the need for competitive analysis
questions.answer: It clarifies which customer needs operations must prioritize
questions.rationale: Different segments value different performance dimensions.

questions.question: Which competitive priority is most directly threatened when variability in delivery times increases?
questions.rationale: Delivery reliability and speed are time based priorities.

questions.question: Order qualifiers are best described as:
options: Minimum standards required to compete in a market
questions.answer: Minimum standards required to compete in a market
questions.rationale: Without qualifiers, a product is not even considered.

questions.question: Which situation best illustrates an order winner rather than an order qualifier?
options: Offering extended warranty and roadside assistance
questions.answer: Offering extended warranty and roadside assistance
questions.rationale: Order winners differentiate firms in customer choice.

questions.question: Why is total factor productivity more difficult to improve than partial productivity?
options: It reflects the combined efficiency of all resources
It focuses only on labor performance
It is not comparable across organizations
questions.answer: It reflects the combined efficiency of all resources
questions.rationale: Improvements must come from system wide changes.

questions.question: An increase in labor productivity accompanied by a decrease in multifactor productivity suggests:
options: Capital and other inputs are being used less efficiently
questions.answer: Capital and other inputs are being used less efficiently
questions.rationale: Multifactor productivity captures trade offs among inputs.

questions.question: Which factor affecting productivity is most closely linked to reducing rework and scrap?
questions.rationale: Higher quality reduces waste and reprocessing.

questions.question: Why must operations strategy be derived rather than independently created?
options: It must align with corporate and business unit strategies
It changes too frequently to stand alone
It only applies to manufacturing firms
questions.answer: It must align with corporate and business unit strategies
questions.rationale: Strategy coherence across levels is essential.

questions.question: Which competitive priority is most likely to conflict with cost leadership if pursued aggressively?
questions.rationale: Customization typically increases cost and complexity.

questions.question: In the hierarchy of operations decisions, tactical decisions primarily translate:
options: Daily activities into long term plans
questions.rationale: Tactical decisions operationalize strategy.

questions.question: Which measure would be most appropriate when comparing productivity across departments using different technologies?
questions.rationale: It accounts for multiple heterogeneous inputs.

questions.question: Environmental scanning contributes to operations strategy primarily by:
options: Identifying shifts in market and competitive conditions
questions.answer: Identifying shifts in market and competitive conditions
questions.rationale: Strategy must adapt to environmental changes.

questions.question: Operations strategy aligns [dropdown_1] with [dropdown_2] to achieve competitive advantage.
questions.rationale: Alignment ensures operations support market requirements.

questions.question: Order qualifiers ensure [dropdown_1], while order winners create [dropdown_2].
questions.rationale: Qualifiers allow competition, winners drive choice.

questions.question: Partial productivity focuses on [dropdown_1], whereas total productivity considers [dropdown_2].
questions.rationale: The scope of inputs differentiates productivity measures.

questions.question: Competitive priorities are driven primarily by ________ expectations.
questions.rationale: Priorities respond to what the market values.

questions.question: The ratio of outputs produced to inputs used is known as ________.
questions.rationale: This ratio defines productivity.

questions.question: A shortfall between competitive priorities and capabilities requires management to ________ the gap.
questions.rationale: Operations strategy aims to close capability gaps.

questions.question: Which dimensions are explicitly identified as competitive priorities in operations strategy?
questions.rationale: These four dimensions define competitive priorities.

questions.question: Which activities belong to market analysis when deriving operations strategy?
questions.rationale: Market analysis focuses on customers and competitors.

questions.question: Which factors can directly improve productivity?
questions.rationale: These factors enhance efficient use of inputs.

questions.question: Match each strategy level with its primary focus.
Match each productivity type with its description.
Match each decision level with an example decision.
Analyze how misidentifying order winners can lead to ineffective operations strategies, using a real or hypothetical firm as context.
Discuss the trade offs operations managers face when attempting to simultaneously improve cost efficiency and flexibility.
Evaluate how productivity measurement can influence strategic and tactical decision making in operations management.
